channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pierre Poilievre on Adam Smith and the Role of Free Markets in Society
item.description: In his article commemorating the 250th anniversary of Adam Smith's 'Wealth of Nations,' Pierre Poilievre argues that free markets are inherently moral and beneficial to society. He clarifies common mi...

item.title: Guest Stars Share Memorable Scenes from 'FPJ's Batang Quiapo'
item.description: The popular TV series 'FPJ's Batang Quiapo' has not only captured viewers' attention with its gripping storylines but also left a lasting impression on its guest stars. In a recent feature, several gu...

item.title: Selected LGUs implement 4-day workweek to save energy amid rising oil prices
item.description: Starting March 9, 2026, several local government units (LGUs) in the Philippines have implemented a four-day workweek in response to the national government's call for energy efficiency due to rising ...

item.title: James Talarico Wins Democratic Nomination for Texas Senate Seat
item.description: State Representative James Talarico emerged victorious in the Texas Senate Democratic primary, defeating fellow Representative Jasmine Crockett. His win positions him as the Democratic candidate likel...

item.title: Behind the Kyle and Jackie ‘O’ Radio Show Dispute and Suspension
item.description: After more than 25 years on air, the Kyle and Jackie O Show has come to an abrupt halt. Kyle Sandilands, one of Australia's most notorious radio personalities, has been suspended due to what has been ...

item.title: Tanzanian Traders Face Challenges Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Tanzanian business people living and working in the Middle East are facing growing anxiety as conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran escalates. Imran, a Tanzanian trader in Dubai dealing mainly in ...

item.title: Parliament Lacks Clear Strategy for Military Deployment Against Gang Violence
item.description: Almost a month after President Cyril Ramaphosa announced that soldiers would be deployed to assist the police in tackling gang violence, Parliament's oversight committees are still waiting for a clear...

item.title: Nedbank Cup Semi-Finals Set: TS Galaxy vs Milford FC, Durban City vs Casric Stars
item.description: The Nedbank Cup semi-finals are shaping up to deliver an exciting mix of top-flight experience and lower-league ambition. TS Galaxy, the only team among the semi-finalists with previous Nedbank Cup su...

item.title: Sifuna and allies boycott Oburu-chaired ODM parliamentary group meeting amid party tensions
item.description: Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna and his allies in the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) have boycotted a parliamentary group meeting convened by Oburu Odinga, the party's acting leader, at State House. Th...

item.title: Aurat March activists released in Islamabad; civil society calls for investigation into treatment during detention
item.description: Islamabad police released 44 women’s rights activists a day after detaining them ahead of the International Women’s Day rally. Police cited Section 144 and the absence of a No Objection Certificate fo...

item.title: UK government monitors economic risks amid Iran conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has cautioned that the ongoing conflict involving Iran could have repercussions for the UK economy. Speaking at a community centre in London, he acknowledged public con...

item.title: Nigerian Investors Tap into Ghana’s High-End Real Estate Market
item.description: Plenty Nigerian investors don dey show interest for Ghana high-end real estate market as dem dey see am both as better investment and lifestyle choice. Devtraco Group don open up im main developments ...

item.title: Understanding the Aussie Stock Market Drop Amid Rising Weapons and Oil Shares
item.description: In the latest segment of Up Late, Ben Harvey analyses the recent $100 billion downturn in the Australian stock market. He explains that a psychological phenomenon known as 'myopic loss aversion' often...

item.title: Court halts relocation of L&J informal settlement residents pending legal approvals
item.description: Business and property owners in Olifantsfontein have successfully obtained an urgent interim interdict from the Gauteng High Court, stopping the relocation of 68 families from the nearby L&J informal ...

item.title: ST families urge district officials to complete housing construction in Tenkasi
item.description: A group of Scheduled Tribe (ST) families residing in the Western Ghats near Sivagiri have approached the Tenkasi district administration seeking urgent completion of housing projects meant for them. T...

item.title: King Charles praises Commonwealth unity at Westminster Abbey service amid calls to remove Prince Andrew from succession
item.description: King Charles has highlighted the Commonwealth's potential as a unifying force in a divided world during his message at the annual Commonwealth Day service held at Westminster Abbey in London. The even...

item.title: Mau Mau Survivors: Scars, Mass Graves and Embodied Memory as Archives of Colonial Violence in Kenya
item.description: Between 1952 and 1963, Kenya face one serious violent time during the Mau Mau uprising against British colonial rule. The thing start from land dispossession and political oppression, turn into full w...

item.title: Highlights from Suliman Carrim’s testimony at the Madlanga Commission
item.description: On day 73 of the Madlanga commission of inquiry, North West businessman Suliman Carrim appeared to give testimony regarding his financial dealings and alleged associations. Carrim applied to present h...

item.title: High school students, teachers, and soldiers plant trees at Camp Manuel T. Yan, Sr. in Davao de Oro
item.description: On March 7, 2026, teachers and high school students from Tuboran National High School, together with soldiers from the Army’s 10th Infantry Division, planted over 700 tree seedlings around Camp Manuel...

item.title: KCMC to Open Dedicated Heart Treatment Unit in 2027 to Serve Northern Zone
item.description: Kilimanjaro Christian Medical Centre (KCMC) in Moshi, Kilimanjaro Region, is planning to open a specialised cardiac treatment unit in 2027 after an investment of over Sh25 billion. Currently, only Jak...
